item.title: Experts bust common food myths, including one that half of all Aussies believe
item.description: It is one of many myths the Food Information Safety Council is looking to bust.

item.title: COVID-19 vaccines carry substantially less risk to kids' heart health than infection, new study finds
item.description: Children who catch COVID-19 are at risk of contracting dangerous heart inflammation, but don't face the same danger from getting vaccinated against the virus.

item.title: REVIEW: As a long-lapsed Pokemon fan, it feels like Legends Z-A was made for me. But this is my only wish for
item.description: REVIEW: Is the franchise headed in the right direction? Yes. Is it the perfect Pokemon game? No.

item.title: Experimental daily pill may lower cholesterol in patients who don't see enough benefit with statins alone
item.description: It is similar to other pills available in Australia already.

item.title: 'Never wash raw chicken': More than half of Australians risking higher chance of food poisoning with common ha
item.description: P﻿eople think this measure is protecting them, but experts warn it could do the opposite.

item.title: Brisbane and Gold Coast residents warned after measles cases detected at concert, tourist attractions
item.description: Residents of two major cities are being told to be on high alert.

item.title: Cost of treatment for deadly cancer slashed dramatically after PBS addition
item.description: Those with multiple myeloma, an incurable blood cancer, would previously pay up to $150,000 for treatment.

item.title: Amyl and the Sniffers' free gig shut down over security fears at Fed Square
item.description: The band paid for $35,000 worth of drink for disappointed fans.

item.title: Parents of teens tragically killed by methanol poisoning team up with government for schoolies abroad warning
item.description: The mothers of two Melbourne teenagers who tragically died last year have teamed up with the government in a warning campaign for young travellers.

item.title: New mRNA flu vaccine far more effective than existing jabs, but has some limitations, study finds
item.description: A newly developed mRNA-based flu vaccine is almost 35 per cent more effective against the most common form of influenza, according to a new study.

item.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
item.description: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game’s producer.

item.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
item.description: A﻿s the under-16 social media ban inches closer, questions are being raised about the implications the ban will have on people who target their content toward younger audiences.

item.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
item.description: Experts from a range of fields, including OpenAI's own wellbeing panel, are worried about what it means for mental health.

item.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
item.description: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

item.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
item.description: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

item.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
item.description: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

item.title: The Oscars to abandon broadcast TV, leaving longtime home of US network ABC
item.description: In a seismic shift for one of television’s marquee events, the Academy Awards will depart broadcast TV.

item.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
item.description: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' dominates SAG's Actor Awards with seven nominations
item.description: One Battle After Another dominated nominations to the Actor Awards, with Paul Thomas Anderson's ragtag revolutionary saga landing a record seven nods in the annual SAG-AFTRA honours.

item.title: A cultural shift is brewing in Australia as non-alcoholic beer sales explode
item.description: Heaps Normal experienced its biggest two-month period of sales in November and December 2025 since its launch in 2020.

item.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
item.description: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

item.title: Australian parcel service Sendle suddenly ceases operations after 12 years
item.description: Sendle, founded in 2014, ﻿sent an email to customers informing them that the service will be halting all bookings "effective immediately" from January 11.

item.title: Online music store Bandcamp bans AI music
item.description: "﻿With this policy, we're putting human creativity first", the store said in a statement this week.

item.title: Cult Australian designer clothing brand Sass & Bide to close down within weeks
item.description: The Myer-owned label said the closure was not goodbye, but a "see you soon".

item.title: Iconic Melbourne retailer Fletcher Jones shutting down
item.description: The announcement is the latest blow to the nation's struggling retail sector.

item.title: Australian billionaires' wealth rose by $600,000 per day in 2025: new report
item.description: A new report has found the average Australian billionaire saw their wealth rise by almost $600,000 per day in 2025, while one in three households faced food insecurity.

item.title: Ampol's $1.1 billion takeover of servo network hits hurdle
item.description: The competition watchdog has failed to approve the deal until further investigation is conducted.

item.title: What's open on the Australia Day public holiday
item.description: Australia Day is a national public holiday but retail workers around the country will still be hard at work.

item.title: Australian dollar cracks 70 US cents on climb to three-year high
item.description: T﻿he local currency today was worth US70 cents, its highest since February 2023.

item.title: Australia's most valuable company continues climb after claiming crown from Commonwealth Bank
item.description: Mining giant BHP has consolidated its new crown as Australia's most valuable company, a day after knocking off the country's biggest bank at the top of the stock exchange.

item.title: Gold price soars to $US5300, as questions surround whether Aussie dollar rally will continue
item.description: E﻿xpects are not ruling out the precious metal hitting the $US6000 mark later this year.

item.title: The biggest video games set to be released this year
item.description: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto...

item.title: The three things that could save Aussies from looming interest rate hell
item.description: There's some hope for mortgage-holders, one of the big banks has said.

item.title: Tesla's sales slowdown puts Elon Musk's enormous pay deal at risk
item.description: Tesla has seen its first decline in annual revenue in the company's history, putting CEO Elon Musk's historic pay package deal at risk.

item.title: 'Cold shower' for mortgage holders: Experts' gloomy rates' forecast
item.description: E﻿conomists are backing the RBA to raise the cash rate next week, and any future relief could be months away.

item.title: Nine sells talkback radio assets to Laundy family for $56 million
item.description: An Aussie pub tycoon is making a venture into the world of the media with a big purchase.

item.title: Dragging Melbourne roadworks threaten to force locals out of business
item.description: Rebekah Becsi fears she'll have to close down her discount food store in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Gold price tumbles after Trump names pick to lead US Federal Reserve
item.description: I﻿nvestors are spurning gold and other precious metals in a vote of confidence for the man expected to lead the world's most powerful central bank.

item.title: Treasurer defends government spending ahead of expected rates hike
item.description: Mortgage holders are bracing for a rates hike today, but Jim Chalmers insists the government isn't to blame.

item.title: Iconic Sydney brewery to shut its doors
item.description: A popular brewery is set to shut its doors ﻿in Sydney's inner west in search of a tree change.

item.title: 'Silent strain': More than a million homeowners struggle with mortgage repayments
item.description: The latest cash rate rise is another hammer blow for struggling mortgage holders, new research has found.

item.description: Here's what you need to know about Mario Tennis Fever, Pragmata and Resident Evil Requiem.

item.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
item.description: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series.

item.title: Rio Tinto scraps $300 billion merger plan with rival Glencore
item.description: B﻿oth companies said any potential deal would fail to deliver shareholders enough value.

item.title: Australian luxury retailer Cosette set to close down after more than 11 years in business
item.description: An Australian luxury retailer is set to close down after more than 11 years in business.

item.title: Deal reached to save collapsed Healthscope private hospitals
item.description: H﻿ealthscope will become a not-for-profit operator in order to save its suite of hospitals after the company collapsed financially last year.

item.title: Australia quietly moves to impose tariffs on Chinese steel imports
item.description: T﻿he move follows concerns by local businesses they are facing unfair competition, but risks retaliation from Beijing.

item.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
item.description: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

item.title: ANZ posts nearly $2 billion in quarterly profit, days after charging staff for 'welcome back' sausage sizzle
item.description: ANZ﻿'s cash profit for the first quarter jumped by six per cent to $1.94 billion, partly driven by the thousands of redundancies announced last year.

item.title: Iconic Australian retailer Barbeques Galore collapses into receivership﻿, 500 jobs at risk
item.description: Around 500 jobs are now at risk.

item.title: Australia Post wants to raise stamp prices
item.description: T﻿he national postal service says it's needed to offset losses in its letter division.

item.description: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

item.description: T﻿he decision hasn't been universally popular, but Minns insists the decision is the right thing to do.

item.title: Higher GST, lower income tax, part of plan to lift Australia's economy
item.description: Strong economic medicine is needed over d﻿ebt levels racked up by Australian states, a global body warns.

item.title: Australian food court chain Sushi Sushi sells to Japanese dining empire in estimated $160 million deal
item.description: Sushi Sushi, which began in Melbourne in 1998, has been snapped ﻿up by Tokyo-based Genki Global Dining Concepts.

item.title: Opposition leader Angus Taylor unveils his new shadow ministry
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has unveiled his new shadow ministry this afternoon, bringing back the Nationals and exiled members.

item.title: Australian gas producer Santos wins court fight over net zero claims
item.description: A lawsuit against gas producer Santos alleged the company misled the public on its plans to achieve net zero carbon emissions.

item.title: Record number of cafes and restaurants going out of business
item.description: T﻿he hospitality industry in Australia is now split between winners and losers, new research shows.

item.title: Today in History - February 18: Bunch of grapes knocks TV reporter unconscious
item.description: A Brazilian television presenter was knocked unconscious on live TV after touching a bunch of grapes on February 18, 1996.

item.description: One of Australia's most prominent conservative senators has delivered a stinging critique of the One Nation leader.

item.description: "Parents are worried about bringing their children to prayers. No community should feel this way in Australia."

item.title: Australian linked to ISIS issued temporary ban from coming home
item.description: T﻿he Australian citizen is one of 34 people who has attempted to return to the country from Syria.

item.title: Man charged over alleged death threats to Treasurer Jim Chalmers
item.description: W﻿eapons were seized from the Sydney man's home after several alleged threatening phone calls to Chalmers' office.

item.title: 'This is peak Australia': NAB chief warns living standards will stagnate without serious reform
item.description: After generations of improvement, our living standards will be stuck in the present – or go backwards – without major economic reform.

item.title: Aussies back tax hike as senator's beer vs gas profit exchange goes viral
item.description: V﻿oters across the political spectrum agree gas exports should be taxed, after David Pocock's shock revelation.

item.title: 'I would ask that you retract that last comment': Jacinta Allan's angry clash with reporter
item.description: An irate ﻿V﻿ictorian Premier Jacinta Allan has briefly stalled a press conference after a reporter refused to retract a claim she appeared "disinterested".

item.description: N﻿SW Fair Trading found the company falsely claimed the proceeds of sales would go to victims and their families.

item.description: T﻿he job cuts have saved $118 million alone.

item.description: e﻿Bay hopes buying Depop will help it reach a younger and more fashion-forward audience.

item.description: A special prosecutor had demanded the death penalty for Yoon, saying his actions posed a threat to the country’s democracy.

item.title: Texts from China's alleged 'spy thriller' ring revealed
item.description: An alleged plot by Chinese citizens to infiltrate a religious network in Australia had "a bit of a spy thriller feel to it", according to court documents.

item.description: H﻿alf of those set to benefit are pensioners.

item.description: H﻿e also accused former President Obama of leaking classified information.

item.title: Trump's made tariffs central to his presidency. Chaos may come next
item.description: Trump had made tariffs the bedrock of his economic pitch to voters ahead of the midterm elections, even describing tariffs as his "favourite word in the dictionary".

item.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he wants a global tariff of 15 per cent.

item.title: The US shut down its Global Entry system amid government chaos. Here's what that means for Aussie travellers
item.description: The US just slammed its Global Entry system shut amid ongoing government chaos.

item.title: 'It's a hard no from me': Premier quashes call for daylight saving in south-east Queensland before Olympics
item.description: ﻿The discussion comes amid efforts to boost Brisbane's night-life economy ahead of the Brisbane 2032 OIympic Games.

item.title: Unions say controversial capital gains tax discount 'hurts workers'
item.description: Australian Unions say they are calling for an overhaul of capital gains tax concessions on property to "help drive a fairer housing system."

item.title: Gunman shot dead at Trump's Florida home of Mar-a-Lago was 'fixated on the Epstein files': report
item.description: An armed man who was shot dead at the US President Donald Trump's residence at Mar-a-Lago in Florida was fixated on the Epstein files, according to TMZ.

item.title: China to be 'on parity' with the United States in nuclear weapons within years, official warns
item.description: "[China] has deliberately, and without constraint, massively expanded its nuclear arsenal."

item.description: Employee shareholders at an Aussie start-up are set for a big pay day after the company was acquired for $1.6 billion.

item.title: High-speed rail hoped to ease housing crisis as government throws $230 million more at plan
item.description: T﻿he federal government has announced an additional $230 million to plan for a ﻿high-speed rail link between Sydney and Newcastle.

item.description: Indignant UK politicians have lined up to criticise the former prince, with some calling to scrap a long-standing protection for the royal family.

item.description: T﻿he software company said ongoing developments in AI would deliver a leaner organisation.

item.description: A﻿nthony Albanese had to be evacuated from his official residence last night.

item.description: Domino's started offering fewer discounts, and some of its customers weren't too happy.

item.description: The incident quickly gained attention, with members of the community saying the man's actions were "pure laziness", "disgusting", and ﻿"such a shame".

item.description: T﻿he meeting at Trump's Florida residence happened just before a $1.5 billion announcement.

item.description: The 39-year-old was accused of putting the United States and Australia at risk thanks to his crimes.

item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced he wants to extend a touted east-coast high-speed rail projec﻿t to Melbourne.

item.title: Trio of US politicians pushing for much higher tariffs on lamb from Australia and New Zealand
item.description: As Australia's sheep farmers began grappling with the impact of President Donald Trump's new round of tariffs, some US politicians were already pushing to make their lives even harder.

item.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
item.description: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

item.title: Glue Store to close in shake-up by retail giant
item.description: The company said it now wants to focus on its global brands.

item.title: PM Anthony Albanese apologises to Grace Tame after using the word 'difficult' to describe her in rapid-fire in
item.description: Former Australian of the Year and child abuse survivor Grace Tame has refused Anthony Albanese's apology after he used the word "difficult" to describe her during an interview.

item.title: Opposition calls for universities to scrap group assignments after fierce student backlash
item.description: "There is always that student who does the work, and that student who reaps the benefit."

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese defiant as Australian children detained in Syria plead to come home
item.description: Anthony Albanese has voiced compassion for the 23 Australian children detained in Syria, but remained defiant against their desperate pleas to return home.

item.title: Qantas unveils major change to frequent flyer program as airline records bumper $1.46 billion profit
item.description: Qantas has announced major changes to its frequent flyer program after posting a bumper $1.46 billion half-year profit.

item.title: Sussan Ley's parting shot to Angus Taylor as she leaves politics after 25 years
item.description: In a statement on Friday morning, Ley announced she had tendered her resignation, effective immediately﻿.﻿

item.description: The company behind one of Australia's most iconic brands is set to be put up for sale as its American owner focuses on operations closer to home.

item.description: Australia's financial watchdog has taken legal action against the insurer behind the Budget Direct products.

item.title: Government won't rule out changes to negative gearing
item.description: Jim Chalmers has effectively confirmed Treasury has looked into the impact of limiting one of Australia's most contentious tax breaks for property investors.

item.title: Former US president Bill Clinton had no knowledge of Epstein crimes, says Hillary Clinton
item.description: The former US secretary of state and first lady was standing by her husband Bill Clinton at the end of a six-hour deposition.

item.description: T﻿he newly-unveiled mid-strength brew signals a growing trend across Australia as illustrated by the sales numbers.

item.description: A 20-year-old man has been charged after allegedly planning a terrorist attack on multiple political and religious sites in Perth.

item.title: 'Will not be mourned': PM's brutal message after confirmed death of Iranian leader

item.title: Dire shortage of healthcare providers capable of performing potentially life-changing endometriosis scans
item.description: A life-changing ultrasound saved her from that fate, but there's a dire shortage of people capable of performing them in Australia.

item.description: T﻿his weekend's strikes by the United States and Israel on Israel come on the back of months of tensions ratcheting up in the Middle East. But the origins go back decades.

item.title: How world leaders have reacted to the US and Israel joint strikes on Iran
item.description: Anthony Albanese and his Canadian counterpart expressed explicit support but most world leaders have been much more cautious.

item.description: O﻿n Saturday, a surprise change of movements by Khamenei. It was an opening some Israeli and US officials believed was too good to pass up.

item.description: The Department of Home Affairs was forced to issue a public warning against temporary visitors applying for ﻿a $50 protection visa (subclass 866).

item.description: Global travel disruptions will stretch into a fourth day with thousands of flights grounded and airports remaining closed as the conflict widens in the Middle East.

item.description: Kuwaiti air defences accidentally shot the F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jets down. All six crew members ejected safely, US Central Command said.

item.description: The city has been caught in the crossfire of US-Iran tensions, with residents and tourists on high alert.

item.description: T﻿he merger of two Hollywood giants will mean big changes to viewing habits globally.

item.description: The company has issued an alert for disruptions to multiple services in the United Arab Emirates ﻿and in Bahrain.

item.description: Donald Trump's attacks on Iran have angered some of his most prominent and influential backers.

item.description: Under the proposed laws, Victorians who are able to do their job from home will have the right to do so at least two days per week.

item.description: The Israel Defence Force has confirmed it has launched strikes on Tehran and Beirut.

item.title: Trump says Iran war could last weeks as Netanyahu defends decision to attack
item.description: The Middle East war continues to intensify, with the US and Israel battering Tehran as Iran expands its targets further across the region.

item.title: Today in History - March 4: Judge orders destruction of one of the best horror films ever made
item.description: A German judge ordered the destruction of all copies of one of the most influential films of all time after its release on March 4, 1922.

item.title: The 39km-wide stretch of water threatening to throw the global economy into turmoil
item.description: Oil prices are surging following the United States' and Israel's attacks on Iran, but not because of Tehran's status as a significant fuel producer.

item.title: The reality of being an Aussie stuck in Dubai during the Middle East crisis
item.description: A stopover in the UAE is often an essential circuit breaker for anyone doing the long haul trip from Australia to Europe, almost a jet lag cheat code, so we thought little of it when we committed to a two-night stay.

item.title: Aussie pubs, jobs safe for now as UK company lays off hundreds of staff

item.description: The average price of unleaded petrol has surged well past $2 a litre in Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane today.

item.title: Labor identity Tim Picton's alleged killer wins bail bid over second assault
item.description: A man accused of fatally assaulting Labor identity Tim Picton will be released from custody after he was granted bail for allegedly attacking another man.

item.description: A﻿ustralian dairy farmers are calling for stricter labelling laws.

item.description: "Any increase to the price has to be passed onto the customer straight away."

item.title: Could this be the 'ultimate constraint' for Trump amid war with Iran?
item.description: When under pressure on inflation, tariffs, immigration and even the Jeffrey Epstein case, the Trump administration has pointed to the success of the financial markets.

item.title: What does the potential end of Kyle and Jackie O's $100 million contracts mean for Australian radio?
item.description: The once bullet-proof radio duo came undone after Jackie 'O' Henderson sensationally quit the KIIS FM show yesterday﻿ following an on-air argument with Kyle Sandilands on February 20.

item.title: The 'good signs' for interest rates after Australia's economy growth stronger than expected
item.description: "The Reser﻿ve Bank, who is watching all of this with concern to interest rates, will like what they see."

item.title: Sydney woman undergoes surgery after being attacked by celebrity dentist who was later shot dead by police
item.description: Dr Steven Lin was on a horror rampage, apparently attacking strangers until one turned the tables on him.

item.title: Israel vows to kill Iran's new supreme leader
item.description: Hello, and welcome to our continuing coverage of the conflict in the Middle East.﻿Israel has been unrelenti...

item.title: Israel targets Iran's security forces and leadership as Iran presses attacks across the region
item.description: The United States and Israel hit Iran's capital and other cities in multiple airstrikes on Wednesday, the fifth day of the war with Iran.

item.title: Cracks appear in Trump's MAGA base as leading figures criticise the Iran war
item.description: For US President Donald Trump, some of the sharpest criticism he's faced in the early days of the Iran war has come from once-loyal media figures far more accustomed to singing his praises.

item.title: 'Sunk by a torpedo': US strikes Iranian warship in first since WWII
item.description: The Sri Lankan Navy said it has recovered dozens of bodies off its coastline.

item.title: Today in History - March 5: Notorious Soviet dictator purportedly murdered to prevent war with US
item.description: Joseph Stalin, the murderous dictator of the Soviet Union, died on March 5, 1953, after suffering a massive stroke.

item.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
item.description: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

item.title: Son of ousted king positions himself to take over in Iran
item.description: He hasn't set foot in the country since he was 18, but there are growing calls to install him as leader.

item.title: Teacher pleads guilty to having sex with 17-year-old student
item.description: She remains on bail under strict conditions which prohibit her from entering any educational institution.

item.title: Chance of double rate-hike looming as Aussie households squeezed
item.description: "Despite the softer January spending figures, we think another rate hike is on the cards for May."

item.title: Sixth person charged for alleged involvement in the shooting and firebombing targeting former NRL player's fam
item.description: The 19-year-old was taken to Bankstown Police Station and charged with a raft of offences in relation to an alleged firebombing.

item.title: Millions of Aussies will get an extra hour of sleep next month as daylight saving ends
item.description: B﻿ut a few states and territories will miss out on the Sunday morning sleep in.

item.title: Australia's nuclear emergency centre 'on alert' over conflict in Iran
item.description: I﻿t comes as Foreign Minister Penny Wong admitted she was "concerned" at the growing conflict.

item.title: US Senate rejects war powers bill to halt attacks against Iran
item.description: US Senate rejects war powers bill to halt attacks against Iran

item.title: Teens charged following alleged kidnapping of woman, 72, last year
item.description: The pair were both charged with a raft of offences.

item.title: Veteran protester's arm broken in altercation with US senator
item.description: A Marine Corps veteran suffered a broken arm as he was dragged out of a hearing in the US Capitol by police and a senator.

item.title: Snorkeller bitten by shark off Australian tourist hotspot
item.description: He was airlifted to hospital and is believed to be in a stable condition.

item.title: Farrer byelection to be held on May 9 after Sussan Ley's resignation from politics
item.description: The byelection will be held on Saturday, May 9, House Speaker Milton Dick announced during Question Time today.

item.title: Car buying platform enters administration, leaving 200 customers without vehicles
item.description: C﻿ar sales platform Car Connect has collapsed into voluntary administration.

item.title: Australia's most trusted and distrusted brands revealed as local favourites dominate
item.description: S﻿ome brands soared and others struggled.

item.title: Australians paid estimated $1.6 billion in unnecessary credit card interest last year, survey claims
item.description: Experts recommend millions of Australians diarise a financial health check every 12 months.

item.title: M﻿elbourne dad James Wright's plea for the ABC to translate Bluey episodes to Auslan has finally been answered
item.description: "For my daughter, it means she can experience these shows in her first language."

item.title: Gang allegedly enlisted teenager in daycare murder plot, court hears
item.description: A teen rugby hopeful's chances of playing professionally are in tatters after allegedly being enlisted into a gang that sought to murder a man outside a daycare centre.

item.title: Man dead after shooting in Melbourne's west
item.description: A﻿nother man remains on the run.

item.title: Thousands of Aussies are watching this flight closely
item.description: With 115,000 Australians in the region when fighting began, the government has moved swiftly to offer assis...

item.title: Sydney private school keeping tabs and alerting parents on students' toilet use using CCTV outside bathrooms
item.description: Some parents are aghast at the messages they're receiving while their kids are at school.

item.title: Two Australian sailors were on board US submarine when it torpedoed Iranian warship
item.description: 9News understands the Australians are on board the American submarine as part of the AUKUS training rotation.

item.title: Ex-Star boss in breach over 'dysfunctional and unethical' culture, court finds
item.description: The former head of casino operator Star breached his company duties by overseeing a "dysfunctional and unethical" culture in which key information about potential criminal risks was withheld, a court has found.

item.title: Accused campground killer Greg Lynn denied bail
item.description: Greg Lynn sought permission to live with his son, after his lawyers argued the trial against him was weak.

item.title: 'Really frightening': Melbourne woman recalls 'appalling' sexual assault as police continue search for offende
item.description: Police have released a computer-generated image of a man believed to be of interest.

item.title: Australian-built AI tool slated to lead revolutionary reduction in breast cancer deaths
item.description: T﻿he predictive AI tool has been trained to predict the likelihood of developing breast cancer in the next four years.

item.title: Out-of-control truck smashes into almost a dozen parked cars on Sunshine Coast
item.description: Nearly a dozen cars have been smashed by an out-of-control truck on Queensland's Sunshine Coast.

item.title: Woman charged after nine-month-old baby found dead at Adelaide home
item.description: An Adelaide woman has been charged after the death of a nine-month-old boy.

item.title: 'Cool off': mum turned away before murder, inquest told
item.description: An inquest has been thrown into disarray after the lawyer for Kelly Wilkinson's family revealed new evidence.

item.title: First planeload of Australians rescued from Middle East land in Sydney
item.description: A planeload of Australians, who had been stranded in Dubai since the start of the most recent conflict in the Middle East on Saturday, have returned home.

item.title: Iran launches wave of attacks, saying US will 'bitterly regret' ship sinking, calls for 'Trump's blood'
item.description: Iran launched a new wave of attacks on Thursday morning at Israeli and American bases and threatened that t...

item.title: Police charge man, release image of alleged getaway driver one year on from deadly arson attack
item.description: A﻿ composite image of the alleged getaway driver has been released as police close in.

item.title: Prime minister confirms Australian 'military assets' deployed to Middle East amid Iran war
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese confirmed the deployment in parliament today, following a historic address by his Canadian counterpart.

item.title: Today in History - March 6: 'I did it for you, Anna': Mother shoots her daughter's murderer in court
item.description: A German mother shot the killer of her young daughter midway through a trial on March 6, 1981.

item.title: Donald Trump says he's replacing Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem with Republican Senator Markwayne Mul
item.description: S﻿he is the first person shown the door in the president's second cabinet.
